title: Reading: The Thief in the Night
learningGoal: Identify and decode various vector verbs (Jaana, Lena, Dena, Daalna, Padna) in a dramatic narrative.
instructions: Read this dramatic short story. Pay close attention to how the author uses compound verbs to show suddenness, force, and finality. Notice what happens when a negative sentence is used.
readingText:
रात के बारह बज रहे थे। अचानक, बाहर से एक ज़ोर की आवाज़ आई। मैं डर गया।
मैंने अपनी पत्नी को जगा दिया। 'उठो! कोई खिड़की तोड़ रहा है!'
हमने देखा कि एक चोर ने सारा कीमती सामान बैग में रख लिया था।
अचानक मेरी पत्नी रो पड़ी। चोर ने उसे डरा दिया था।
मैंने हिम्मत की और पुलिस को फोन कर दिया। पुलिस तुरंत आ गई।
पुलिस ने चोर को पकड़ लिया। चोर ने गुस्से में बैग ज़मीन पर फेंक डाला।
लेकिन उसने किसी को नहीं मारा। पुलिस उसे ले गई, और हम चैन से सो गए।
textInTargetLanguage:
रात के बारह बज रहे थे। अचानक, बाहर से एक ज़ोर की आवाज़ आई। मैं डर गया।
मैंने अपनी पत्नी को जगा दिया। 'उठो! कोई खिड़की तोड़ रहा है!'
हमने देखा कि एक चोर ने सारा कीमती सामान बैग में रख लिया था।
अचानक मेरी पत्नी रो पड़ी। चोर ने उसे डरा दिया था।
मैंने हिम्मत की और पुलिस को फोन कर दिया। पुलिस तुरंत आ गई।
पुलिस ने चोर को पकड़ लिया। चोर ने गुस्से में बैग ज़मीन पर फेंक डाला।
लेकिन उसने किसी को नहीं मारा। पुलिस उसे ले गई, और हम चैन से सो गए।
baseLanguageSupportText:
It was twelve o'clock at night. Suddenly, a loud sound came from outside. I became scared (fear took over).
I woke my wife up. 'Get up! Someone is breaking the window!'
We saw that a thief had kept all the valuable items in the bag (for himself).
Suddenly my wife burst into tears. The thief had scared her.
I gathered courage and called the police. The police arrived immediately.
The police caught the thief. The thief, in anger, threw the bag violently on the ground.
But he did not hit anyone. The police took him away, and we fell asleep peacefully.

glossary.baseTranslation: became scared (Darna + Jaana: Completed state of fear)

exampleSentence.targetText: कुत्ते को देखकर मैं डर गया।
exampleSentence.transliteration: Kutte ko dekh kar main dar gaya.
exampleSentence.baseTranslation: Seeing the dog, I became scared.

glossary.baseTranslation: woke up (outward action to someone else)
caught (Pakadna + Lena: To take into custody)
threw violently (Phenkna + Daalna: Action with force)
did not hit/kill (Notice: No compound verb because it's negative)

sentenceSupportPairs.targetText: मैंने अपनी पत्नी को जगा दिया।
sentenceSupportPairs.transliteration: Maine apni patni ko jaga diya.

sentenceSupportPairs.baseTranslation: Suddenly my wife burst into tears.

sentenceSupportPairs.targetText: चोर ने गुस्से में बैग ज़मीन पर फेंक डाला।
sentenceSupportPairs.transliteration: Chor ne gusse mein bag zameen par phenk daala.
sentenceSupportPairs.baseTranslation: The thief, in anger, threw the bag violently on the ground.

comprehensionQuestions.questionText: Why did the author write 'Jaga diya' (Woke up) using the vector 'diya' (gave)?
options: Because the action of waking was directed outward, toward his wife.
Because he gave her a gift.

comprehensionQuestions.questionText: When the wife cries, the verb is 'Ro padi'. What nuance does 'padi' add?
options: It means she burst into tears suddenly and involuntarily.
It means she cried on the floor.

comprehensionQuestions.questionText: Why did the author use 'Rakh liya' instead of 'Rakh diya' for the thief putting things in the bag?
options: Because 'liya' (took) indicates he was keeping the items for his own benefit.
Because 'diya' is only for past tense.
Because the bag belonged to the thief.

comprehensionQuestions.questionText: How do we know the thief threw the bag forcefully/violently?
options: The vector verb 'daala' is used (phenk daala), which implies force or recklessness.
He threw it at the police.

comprehensionQuestions.questionText: Look at the sentence 'Lekin usne kisi ko nahi maara'. Why is there NO vector verb attached to 'maara'?
options: Because it is a negative sentence ('nahi'). Compound verbs are dropped in negative sentences.
Because 'maarna' cannot be a compound verb.
Because he didn't hit them very hard.

correctAnswers.rq_1: Because the action of waking was directed outward, toward his wife.
correctAnswers.rq_2: It means she burst into tears suddenly and involuntarily.
correctAnswers.rq_3: Because 'liya' (took) indicates he was keeping the items for his own benefit.
correctAnswers.rq_4: The vector verb 'daala' is used (phenk daala), which implies force or recklessness.
correctAnswers.rq_5: Because it is a negative sentence ('nahi'). Compound verbs are dropped in negative sentences.

feedbackRules.onPass: Astounding! You have cracked the code of Hindi's subtle emotional vocabulary.
feedbackRules.onFail: Review the glossary carefully. Notice the second word in each compound (diya, liya, gaya, padi, daala). That second word tells you HOW the action happened.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good effort. Pay close attention to question 5. The rule of dropping the compound verb in a negative sentence is an absolute law in Hindi.

audioAssets.audioTranscript: रात के बारह बज रहे थे। अचानक, बाहर से एक ज़ोर की आवाज़ आई। मैं डर गया। मैंने अपनी पत्नी को जगा दिया। 'उठो! कोई खिड़की तोड़ रहा है!' हमने देखा कि एक चोर ने सारा कीमती सामान बैग में रख लिया था। अचानक मेरी पत्नी रो पड़ी। चोर ने उसे डरा दिया था। मैंने हिम्मत की और पुलिस को फोन कर दिया। पुलिस तुरंत आ गई। पुलिस ने चोर को पकड़ लिया। चोर ने गुस्से में बैग ज़मीन पर फेंक डाला। लेकिन उसने किसी को नहीं मारा। पुलिस उसे ले गई, और हम चैन से सो गए।

hintText: Look at the ending of 'phenk daala'. 'Daala' literally means poured/put, but here it acts as an amplifier, meaning 'violently'.
commonMistakes: Translating 'Rakh liya' as 'Kept and took' instead of 'Kept (for himself)'.
